buyers.thesis: CMC completed $675M acquisition of Concrete Pipe & Precast (CP&P) in Dec 2025 and acquired Foley Products — building one of the largest precast platforms in the US. Wieser adds critical Midwest/Wisconsin manufacturing capacity to their growing platform.

Acquired both International Concrete Products (Germantown WI, Dec 2023) and Architectural Precast Inc (Browntown WI, April 2024). Actively rolling up Wisconsin/Midwest precast. Wieser is a natural next acquisition for geographic consolidation.

Acquired Boughton's Precast (Pueblo CO) in Feb 2026 — their 3rd acquisition in 6 years after Geneva Pipe & Precast (2020) and ParkUSA (2021). Actively building national precast/pipe platform. Wieser adds Midwest manufacturing.

Oldcastle Infrastructure acquired Concrete Specialties to secure Midwest foothold. CRH (parent) is a $35B building materials company actively consolidating precast. Wieser fills Wisconsin/upper Midwest territory.

Acquired Benton Precast LLC (Conway SC) in Dec 2024, expanding geographic footprint. Growing precast platform through acquisition. Wieser's Wisconsin operations would give Alcrete Midwest manufacturing capability.

Knife River is a major aggregates and construction materials company spun off from MDU Resources. Active in ready-mix and precast across the Upper Midwest. Wieser adds precast capability to their materials platform.
buyers.source: Industry knowledge - Knife River Midwest operations

buyers.thesis: Forterra is a major precast pipe and concrete products manufacturer. Quikrete Holdings took Forterra private. Wieser's Wisconsin precast operations fill a geographic gap in their Midwest coverage.
Just sold CP&P to CMC for $675M. Has proven ability to build and exit precast platforms. May deploy proceeds into a new Midwest-focused precast roll-up with Wieser as anchor platform.
buyers.source: Exa - Eagle/ECPP CP&P exit to CMC

buyers.thesis: Major Midwest precast manufacturer based in Minnesota. Geographic adjacency to Wieser in Wisconsin. Combining operations would create dominant Upper Midwest precast platform with significant cost synergies.
buyers.source: Industry knowledge - Wells Concrete Midwest position

buyers.thesis: Summit Materials (NYSE: SUM) is a $4B+ construction materials company that acquired Argos USA for $3.2B. Expanding concrete/precast capabilities through M&A. Wieser adds precast manufacturing to their materials portfolio.
buyers.source: Industry knowledge - Summit Materials acquisition strategy
